text: Abstract Deep learning generative models recently gain tremendous advancements in the fields of time series. The generative models using deep neural networks for working with time series data heavily rely on the amount and quality of the training data. Training data for time series is usually limited and have certain constraints that need to be satisfied. Thus, augmenting the amount of training data is a necessity. This can be done in three ways, namely by introducing noise to the data, by introducing certain permutations in the data and by generating new synthetic data. In this manuscript, we present a comprehensive literature review to summarize the current state of the art in this domain. All existing works are summarized and categorized in a taxonomy that we have developed for this purpose. In addition, the performance of the different works are evaluated and the evaluation criteria are highlighted. The most encountered challenges are discussed. The main aim is to provide a summary of the generated time series models that can guide researchers with the advancement of the better performing models.
headline: Fully Human Written
